items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Good, I've read the full spec. Let me now examine reference files to understand existing patterns.
Now I have a solid understanding of the patterns. Let me check one more critical thing, then start building.
Good, all the necessary UI components are available through `@salesforce/retail-react-app/app/components/shared/ui`. Now I have everything I need. Let me create all four files.
Now let me create the unit tests. First, the QuickViewModal tests:
Now the ProductTile override tests:
Now let me run the unit tests to verify they pass:
The test utility pulls in transitive ESM dependencies. I need to adjust the approach — either update the jest config or write a lightweight test wrapper.
I need to extend the transformIgnorePatterns to also transform the ESM-only `@salesforce/cc-datacloud-typescript` package:
The `renderWithProviders` utility has deep transitive dependencies. I'll create a lightweight test wrapper instead.
I need to import `@testing-library/jest-dom` for the DOM matchers. Let me check the setup:
I need to add `@testing-library/jest-dom` import. Let me update both test files:
All 12 QuickViewModal tests pass with 100% coverage. Now let's run the ProductTile tests:

The `quick-view-modal` is a new component (not an override of a base component), so it can't be imported via `@salesforce/retail-react-app/...`. I need to use a relative import in the ProductTile override:
Now update the test mock path too:
All 13 ProductTile tests pass. Let me run both test suites together to confirm:
All 25 tests pass. There's 1 failed suite but let me check what it is:
The failing test is the existing E2E test, not our unit tests. All 25 unit tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server can build and resolve our modules:

The dev server builds and returns HTTP 200. However, I notice a warning about `Skeleton` export:  ``` export 'Skeleton' (imported as 'ProductTileSkeleton') was not found in '@salesforce/retail-react-app/app/components/product-tile' ```  This is because our override only exports `default` but the base ProductTile also exports a `Skeleton` named export. Let me check and fix this:
I need to re-export the `Skeleton` from the base module. Let me update the product-tile override:
Now let me re-run tests to make sure everything still passes, and then validate the dev server again:
All 25 tests pass. Let me verify the dev server builds cleanly without the Skeleton warning:
Dev server returns HTTP 200 and the Skeleton warning is gone. The build is clean. Let me now do the SSR safety checklist and perform the commit:
All SSR safety checks pass. Now let me verify the files are all properly created:
Everything looks good. Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:
Now complete the pipeline:

The **storefront-dev** phase for **product-quick-view** is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## Implementation Summary  ### Files Created (4 new files)  | File | Description | |---|---| | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | QuickViewModal component — Chakra Modal wrapping `ProductView` with loading/error states | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | 12 unit tests covering modal shell, content rendering, accessibility | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ProductTile override — wraps base tile with Quick View overlay bar | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | 13 unit tests covering bar rendering, interaction, visual states |  ### Files Modified (1) | File | Change | |---|---| | `jest.config.js` | Added `@salesforce/cc-datacloud-typescript` to `transformIgnorePatterns` for ESM compatibility |  ### Key Design Decisions  1. **Relative import** for QuickViewModal (`../quick-view-modal`) since it's a new component, not a base template override 2. **Re-exported `Skeleton`** from base ProductTile to prevent webpack build warnings 3. **Lightweight test wrapper** instead of `renderWithProviders` from base template (avoids deep transitive ESM dependency issues) 4. **All `data-testid` attributes** per spec: `quick-view-modal`, `quick-view-btn`, `quick-view-spinner`, `quick-view-error`, `product-tile-container`  ### Verification Results - ✅ **25/25 unit tests passing** (100% statement coverage) - ✅ **Dev server HTTP 200** — webpack resolves all modules correctly - ✅ **SSR safety** — no `window`/`document` access, no hydration mismatch risks - ✅ **No `networkidle` patterns** in E2E tests

messages: Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the draft PR.
PR created successfully. Now let me complete the pipeline and commit.
